prompt:
Ultra-realistic portrait of a beautiful Korean woman standing at her desk, in the quiet act of packing up — one book held loosely against her chest with both arms wrapped around it, head turned to look out the classroom window one final time before leaving. She is not looking at anything specific. It is the look of someone memorizing a room. Camera positioned at medium distance, capturing her from mid-thigh up, centered between the window light and the classroom.
Wearing a soft sage green linen button-down shirt tucked loosely into high-waisted dark rust-brown wide-leg trousers, a simple brown woven belt. Tan suede ankle boots. A large worn leather tote bag half-packed on the desk beside her, a scarf tied to the handle. Thin layered gold chains at the collarbone. Long dark brown hair down, loose natural waves, a simple center part.
The golden afternoon light has taken on the deep amber quality of late-hour sun — now warm and almost melancholy, the longest shadows of the day cutting across the rows of empty desks. The window she looks toward is a rectangle of burning amber against the quieting classroom. Her sage linen shirt catches the warm light differently from every direction — cool shadow on one side, warm amber on the other.
35mm analog film grain, Kodak Portra 400 — sage green and rust brown in deeply harmonized Portra earth tones, the melancholy of late golden hour fully expressed in color. Hyper-realistic linen texture, leather bag wear detail. Shallow depth of field. Mood: the end of something unnamed, time passing without drama, the most quietly devastating composition in the series.
